af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Comprehensive weather data API. Simple API key as query parameter. Docs are good but the 2-hour activation delay on new keys is a real gotcha for agents. One Call API 3.0 now requires credit card registration even for free tier. Rate limits (daily + per-minute) documented but the per-minute limit catches agents off guard.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key per account with no fine-grained scopes. API key passed as query parameter (vs header) is slightly less secure. Maintained commercial service. No sensitive data handled — public weather information only.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. API v2.5 and v3.0 stable but the migration from free v3.0 to requiring credit card was disruptive. Multiple data sources backing the service. Minimal retry guidance. Widely used at scale — reliability is generally good in practice.

af_score_components.notes: Has built-in MCP server (port 1337). macOS-only local AI edge runtime with MLX support and OpenAI/Anthropic API compatibility. Primary docs via GitHub README. Auth uses osk-v1 API tokens stored in macOS Keychain — reasonable design. No rate limits as a local service. Error handling is basic for an early project.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local service — TLS applies to outbound cloud API routes. macOS Keychain for credential storage is best practice. EIP-191 signature-based agent identity is a thoughtful design. Remote provider API keys stored securely in Keychain. Early project so dependency hygiene needs ongoing attention.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — reliability depends on user's Mac. No SLA. Early project with limited version history. Requires macOS 15.5+ (16+ for Apple Foundation Models). Port 1337 conflicts possible. Reliability will improve as the project matures.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (WebSocket:3030, HTTP:3031). Plan visualization tool for AI coding agents — renders execution plans as interactive flowcharts before code is written. No authentication (local only) — auth_complexity_score=100. Good README docs. Error handling is basic. Not suitable for headless/CI pipelines.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local WebSocket/HTTP server with no TLS and no authentication by design. Appropriate for local dev tool but means any process on the machine can interact with the API. No secrets needed. npm package dependencies should be audited. Open source with MIT/similar license.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local development tool, no SLA. npx invocation downloads package each run. Port conflicts on 3030/3031 not auto-resolved. Early open source project with limited version history. Reliability appropriate for a dev tool use case.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Merchant-of-record billing platform with REST API, OpenAPI spec, and SDKs. Simple API key authentication (sandbox + production keys). Paddle Billing (v2) vs Paddle Classic are two different APIs — agents must use correct version. Retry guidance not prominently documented. Good SDK support in Python/JS/PHP/Go.

security_score_components.security_notes: Payments require HTTPS — enforced. API key authentication with sandbox/production isolation. PCI-DSS compliant as merchant of record. No fine-grained scope control (API key grants full access). Webhook HMAC-SHA256 signature verification. Secrets via environment variables recommended.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page and SLA for payment processing. Paddle Billing v2 is stable. Migration from Classic to Billing was a significant breaking change. Cursor-based pagination. Retry guidance minimal. As a payment processor, uptime is a core business requirement.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Incident management platform with excellent developer docs. auth_complexity_score=55 — three auth models (REST API keys, user tokens, Events API integration keys) create confusion for agents. Events API (for triggering alerts) is entirely separate from REST API (for management). Retry guidance documented. Dedup keys for idempotency are critical.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. REST API keys + OAuth 2.0 with scopes for third-party access. Events API uses service-level integration keys for isolation. SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA compliant. Webhook signing with HMAC. Secrets via env vars. Enterprise-grade security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: status.pagerduty.com, SLA for enterprise plans. Stable v2 REST API with good deprecation policy. Retry guidance documented. Dedup key mechanism via Events API prevents duplicate incidents from retrying agents. Critical infrastructure tool — reliability is core to their value proposition.

af_score_components.notes: Has official MCP server (pandadoc-mcp on GitHub). Document automation and e-signature platform. API key for server-side, OAuth 2.0 for user-context operations. OpenAPI spec and SDKs available. Document creation is NOT idempotent — retry creates duplicates. API access requires Business plan or higher. Template variable case-sensitivity is a common gotcha.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key + OAuth 2.0 dual auth model. Webhook HMAC-SHA256 signatures. OAuth scopes for document-level access control. SOC2 certified, ESIGN/UETA compliant. Secrets via environment variables. Solid security posture for document handling.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2 certification. Stable API version. Document creation non-idempotency creates reliability risk on retry — agents must implement dedup logic. Cursor-based pagination. Webhook events must be explicitly subscribed. API document limits per plan tier.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server. Multi-source academic paper search aggregating arXiv, PubMed, bioRxiv, medRxiv, Google Scholar, IACR, Semantic Scholar. Optional Semantic Scholar API key via env var — otherwise no auth needed. Good for literature review workflows. Error handling is basic. No pagination for large result sets. Sci-Hub inclusion has legal considerations.

security_score_components.security_notes: Relies on upstream academic APIs' HTTPS. Optional API key via env var (good practice). Mostly open data sources — no sensitive data handled. Open source — dependency audit recommended. Sci-Hub integration raises legal/compliance concerns in some jurisdictions.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on multiple upstream sources (arXiv, PubMed, etc.). Google Scholar scraping can be blocked/rate-limited unpredictably. Planned features (IEEE, ACM) not yet implemented. No formal versioning or reliability documentation. Early open source project.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server with 3 tools: generate_diagram, generate_plot, evaluate_diagram. Generates publication-quality academic figures from text using LLM vision models. Requires OpenAI, Gemini, or Azure OpenAI API keys — adds credential complexity. Auto-refine mode can consume many API calls per diagram. Error handling is basic. Community project, not affiliated with original paper authors.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for outbound LLM API calls. API keys for LLM providers required — stored in .env file (acceptable but Keychain/vault preferred). No sensitive data processed beyond diagram generation. Community project — verify dependencies before production use. Python 3.10+ required.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on LLM provider uptime and API quotas. Auto-refine mode unpredictability means output quality and API cost vary. No formal reliability documentation. Early community project with limited version history. Non-idempotent by nature (generative).

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Chrome extension + self-hosted backend for AI-assisted writing in Overleaf. Cannot be integrated by AI agents without the Chrome extension and browser session. JWT + OAuth authentication with self-hosted Go/MongoDB backend. AGPL-3.0 license has copyleft implications. Not suitable for programmatic agent use — designed for browser-based Overleaf workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted deployment means TLS quality varies by operator. JWT + OAuth authentication. OpenAI API key required in backend config. AGPL-3.0 license — verify compliance for commercial use. No formal security audit. Operator responsible for securing MongoDB and backend service.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Requires Chrome browser and active Overleaf session. Depends on OpenAI API availability. Early project with limited production track record. AI suggestions are read-only (no direct document modification). Reliability appropriate for academic writing tool.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Simple cloud log management with REST API. Single API token in X-Papertrail-Token header — very simple auth. Search uses simple query syntax (not Elasticsearch DSL) limiting filtering power. Results capped at 2000 events — cursor pagination needed for more. API is read-only for log data; ingestion uses separate syslog/HTTP mechanism.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for API and log ingestion. Single API token grants full account access — no scope granularity. SOC2 certified (Solarwinds-owned service). Token via Authorization header/env var. Log data may contain sensitive application data — access control limited to account-level.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Hosted service with status page. Mature, stable API. SOC2 compliance. Log retention limit (1 year max) is a known constraint. Cursor-based pagination for large result sets. Minimal retry guidance. Mature service with good reliability track record.
